name: PLACENTAL ABRUPTION
definition: PLACENTAL HEMORRHAGE premature separation of placenta from myometrium ← maternal hemorrhage into decidua ABRUPTIO PLACENTAE accumulation of blood beneath placenta separating placenta from myometrium MARGINAL PLACENTAL HEMORRHAGE SUBMEMBRANOUS PLACENTAL HEMORRHAGE separation of chorionic membrane from decidua with accumulation of blood in BREUS MOLE = SUBCHORIAL HEMORRHAGE = MASSIVE SUBCHORIAL THROMBOSIS variant of placental abruption with progressive slow intracotyledonary bleeding massive retroplacental hematoma dissecting into placenta; related to placental infarcts

demographics.Etiology: massive pooling + stasis ← extensive venous obstruction

distribution: focal mass of varying echogenicity within placenta
discriminating_features: contiguous retroplacental area of decreased enhancement forming acute angles with
areas of contrast extravasation in infarcted placenta (occasionally)
increased intrauterine T1 signal at site of bleeding
thickened heterogeneous appearing placenta (hematoma of similar echogenicity as placenta)

US: hyperechoic / isoechoic hematoma (initially difficult to distinguish from placenta):
abnormally thick + heterogeneous placenta (if blood isoechoic)
hypoechoic / complex collection between uterine wall + placenta in 50% within 1 week
NECT: high-attenuation blood products in dependent portions of the amniotic sac
CECT: contiguous retroplacental area of decreased enhancement forming acute angles with
areas of contrast extravasation in infarcted placenta (occasionally)
increased intrauterine T1 signal at site of bleeding
thickened heterogeneous appearing placenta (hematoma of similar echogenicity as placenta)
placental margin detached from adjacent myometrium (60%):
hematoma contiguous with placental margin (100%) = marginal hematoma
predominant hemorrhage often separate from placenta, even on opposite side of placenta
total loss of normal placental architecture
gelatinous character of placenta elicited by fetal movement / abdominal jostling
focal mass of varying echogenicity within placenta

clinical.Prognosis: clinically significant if 30-40% of maternal surface of placenta involved
placental membranes are more easily stripped from myometrium than from placenta
